articles.description: Federal authorities charged 10 Southern California defendants in healthcare fraud schemes involving nearly $270 million in fraudulent Medi-Cal claims.

articles.title: Human-AI teams improve healthcare only when clinicians stay in control
articles.description: A scoping review of 140 empirical studies found that human-AI collaboration in healthcare reports the most consistent benefits in diagnostic interpretation, especially when AI supports bounded tasks and clinicians retain final responsibility. Evidence remains fragmented across triage, treatment planning, and documentation, with few studies measuring patient outcomes, system impact, trust calibration, or governance in real-world settings.

articles.title: Clarkson's health dilemma 'common among farmers'
articles.description: The TV star shared his worried treatment would overlap with a busy period for his Cotswolds farm.

articles.description: Kenya’s Health Minister has stopped the construction of a US Ebola facility in Kenya.

articles.title: The alcoholic beverages that will do the least damage to your diet
articles.description: Alcohol is an obstacle to weight loss. But anyone building a balanced menu can also enjoy a cocktail every now and then – you just need to pay attention to what you are putting into your glass.

articles.title: Seer Robotics makes market debut, says Hong Kong is 'gateway' for embodied AI
articles.description: Shanghai-based robotics maker Seer Robotics – best known for providing the "brains" for robots – debuts as a public company in Hong Kong. Emily Tan sits down with COO Jonathan Fan about the choice to list on HKEX, and the company's business model.

articles.title: Lost weight is less likely to return when exercise follows obesity treatment
articles.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 11 randomized controlled trials found that exercise during the post-weight-loss maintenance phase reduced weight regain by an average of 2.81 kg compared with control groups. Evidence for fat-mass reduction remained inconclusive, highlighting the need for longer, standardized trials with better adherence and body-composition monitoring.

articles.title: Longer trading hours unlikely to unlock South Korea's MSCI upgrade: BofA
articles.description: BofA Global Research's Benson Wu says extending South Korea's trading hours alone will not be enough to attract global investors, arguing that broader market-access reforms are needed for the country to achieve MSCI developed-market status. Despite this, he remains constructive on South Korean equities, viewing the market as a key beneficiary of the global AI investment cycle.

articles.title: CalViva Health delivers over $800K in emergency relief funding to local food and shelter providers
articles.description: The health plan, which serves more than 400,000 members in the region, delivered $803,100 through a multiday Community Relief Tour.

articles.title: Study challenges a common belief about vitamin D and sunlight
articles.description: A study of nearly 300 people across northern Britain found that vitamin D levels often stay low all year in groups most at risk. Surprisingly, summer sunshine did not significantly boost vitamin D levels among older adults or people from minoritized ethnic backgrounds.

articles.title: Health care fraud investigation nets a dozen arrests in North Texas
articles.description: Physicians, nurses and other health care workers face an array of federal charges, from conspiracy to commit fraud to offering bribes.

articles.title: Forget Prime Day — The North Face fleeces are up to 72% off at REI right now
articles.description: The best Amazon Prime Day deals are apparently not on Amazon, but REI. For example, this $29 The North Face fleece is an absolute steal.

articles.title: Study finds no detectable short-term brain harm after one youth soccer season
articles.description: A prospective European cohort study found that one season of competitive youth soccer was not associated with detectable short-term differences in cognition, behavior, balance, MRI measures, or blood biomarkers compared with noncontact sports. However, soccer players had higher preseason tNAA, GFAP, and NfL levels than controls, highlighting the need for larger, multiyear studies to clarify whether repeated heading has cumulative effects.

articles.title: ‘Good teeth, you’re deadly inside and outside’: here’s what Indigenous people told us about oral health
articles.description: Good oral health is more than having healthy teeth and gums. Indigenous people told researchers why it’s central to their wider health and wellbeing.

articles.description: A moratorium on new licensing and Nevada Medicaid enrollment for most prospective hospice facilities and home-health providers has taken effect.

articles.description: The cost of a hair transplant is usually the first thing people look up long before they think about clinics or techniques.
